findings.evidence: `rg -n "normalizerQuotient.*lift|QuotientGroup\.lift" code/TauCeti/Algebra/Group/NormalizerQuotient.lean` has no implementation hits, while Mathlib provides `QuotientGroup.lift` and `lift_mk'` at `mathlib/Mathlib/GroupTheory/QuotientGroup/Defs.lean:248` and `:256`.
findings.fix: Add a public `normalizerQuotientLift` (with `@[simp]` mk and comp lemmas, and injective/surjective variants if needed) characterizing maps out of the normalizer quotient.
findings.issue: `normalizerQuotient` has constructor/equality/kernel facts but no named lift/recursion theorem for homomorphisms out of `N(H)/H`, so downstream users must use `QuotientGroup.lift` on `H.subgroupOf (Subgroup.normalizer (H : Set G))`.

summary: The quotient wrapper still lacks the universal-property API needed to define maps out of `N(H)/H` without unfolding the implementation subgroup.
